title: Space Force plans nationwide network of ‘resilient operations centers’
summary: The U.S. Space Force is planning to establish a nationwide network of resilient operations centers to ensure military space missions can continue during wartime attacks. These facilities aim to enhance the resilience and survivability of critical space operations.

title: Observable Space raises $90 million and wins Space Force contract for optical systems
summary: Observable Space raised $90 million and secured a U.S. Space Force contract to develop optical systems for laser communications and space domain awareness.

title: Setting up a permanent lunar presence needs investment in biology
summary: Establishing a permanent human presence on the Moon requires significant investment in biological research. The Apollo 17 mission in 1972 marked the last time humans walked on the lunar surface.

title: Revolv Space enters in-orbit servicing market with Infinite Orbits deal
summary: Revolv Space has entered the in-orbit servicing market by securing a deal with Infinite Orbits. The agreement involves Revolv Space's Solar Array Drive Assemblies (SADAs) for managing satellite solar panels.

title: Germany pushes European military space command initiative
summary: Germany advocates for a unified European approach to military space operations by proposing a European Space Component Command hosted in the country. This initiative aims to enhance coordination among European nations in space defense.

title: CEO Series: Lux Aeterna’s Brian Taylor on what’s next for on-orbit servicing
summary: Lux Aeterna's CEO Brian Taylor discusses the challenges of disposable satellites and the potential of on-orbit servicing in an interview with Space Minds. The conversation highlights future advancements in satellite maintenance and sustainability.

title: The Propulsion Imperative Behind Golden Dome
summary: Golden Dome shifts missile defense focus to propulsion within its distributed infrastructure. The article highlights the importance of propulsion in the broader architecture of missile defense systems.

title: Europe’s biggest space opportunity comes after launch
summary: Europe's space industry is shifting focus from sovereignty debates to launchers, constellations, and national presence at major summits. The upcoming launch presents a significant opportunity for the region.

title: Virgin Galactic returns Unity to flight to prepare for next-generation spaceplane
summary: Virgin Galactic's Unity spaceplane has resumed flights to train pilots for its next-generation spacecraft. The return to service is part of preparations for future missions.

title: FAA requires mishap investigation into latest Starship launch
summary: The FAA has mandated a mishap investigation into SpaceX's latest Starship test flight, delaying its next launch until the probe is completed. The investigation aims to determine the cause of the incident and ensure future flights meet safety standards.

title: Companies like SpaceX want electromagnetic catapults on the moon. Could they be used as weapons?
summary: Companies like SpaceX are exploring the use of electromagnetic catapults on the Moon, which could have strategic and security implications. A report suggests these mass drivers might be repurposed as first-strike weapons systems.

title: Andy Weir's 'The Martian' just got a deluxe edition to celebrate the book's 15-year anniversary, and it's a stunner
summary: Andy Weir's 'The Martian' has been released as a deluxe edition to celebrate its 15-year anniversary. The book, which preceded 'Project Hail Mary,' is being re-released with a stunning makeover.

title: NASA is hatching a 'fast-paced plan' to boost this space telescope. But first, they'll have to find it
summary: NASA is planning to boost the orbit of its Swift space telescope using a private spacecraft, requiring precise predictions of its current Earth orbit. The mission aims to extend the telescope's operational lifespan by increasing its altitude.

title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers a black hole that formed before its host galaxy. Scientists aren't sure how
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope discovered a black hole that formed before its host galaxy, challenging existing theories about the relationship between black holes and galaxies. This finding could lead to a paradigm shift in astrophysics.

title: Astrophotographer spends 115 hours capturing Horsehead Nebula in striking detail with backyard telescope
summary: An astrophotographer spent 115 hours capturing the Horsehead Nebula in stunning detail using a backyard telescope. The image highlights the nebula's dark silhouette against glowing clouds in the constellation Orion.

title: Astronaut captures aurora magic from the ISS | Space photo of the day for May 28, 2026
summary: An astronaut aboard the International Space Station (ISS) captured a stunning photograph of auroras, showcasing the beauty of space weather phenomena. The image was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for May 28, 2026.

title: How to catch the perfect full moonrise — just in time for the Blue Moon show on May 30
summary: A rare Blue Moon will occur on May 30, and timing is crucial to observe its full moonrise. The article provides guidance on how to plan for the perfect viewing.

title: What's next for SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket after its historic debut flight?
summary: SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket completed a successful debut flight last week. The next steps for the next-generation vehicle are being evaluated.

title: Most powerful 'ghost particle' ever may have come from a cosmic particle accelerator fed by a black hole
summary: The most energetic neutrino ever detected may have originated from a blazer powered by a black hole, indicating these cosmic events act as particle accelerators. This discovery suggests black holes play a key role in accelerating high-energy particles in the universe.

title: Enjoy the Season 5 finale of 'For All Mankind' and be first to view spinoff series 'Star City' anywhere with these exclusive VPN deals — save 87% off and a bonus free $30 Amazon Gift Card
summary: A promotion for the Season 5 finale of 'For All Mankind' and its spinoff series 'Star City' offers exclusive VPN deals with Surfshark, including an 87% discount and a $30 Amazon Gift Card.

title: NASA’s X-59 Prepares for First Supersonic Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic research aircraft is preparing for its first supersonic flight and other key test objectives. The upcoming flights mark a significant milestone for the experimental X-plane.

title: I Am Artemis: Daniel Stubbs
summary: NASA's Artemis human landing systems may face visibility issues during lunar landings due to dust clouds, as explained by aerospace engineer Daniel Stubbs. This problem is likened to driving through a cloud of dust that obscures vision.

title: New Landsat Science Team Holds First In-Person Meeting
summary: The new Landsat Science Team held its first in-person meeting from May 5 to 7 at the EROS Center in Sioux Falls, SD. The event introduced the 2026–2030 Science Team members, co-moderated by Chris Neigh.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4900-4907: Pasadena, We Have a Drill Sample!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover successfully drilled a sample at 'Campo Marte' on Mars, as confirmed by downlink data. The rover's latest drill attempt follows a previous operation at 'Atacama' and is part of ongoing scientific exploration.

title: NASA Uses Mineralogical Marker to Understand Ancient Martian Climate
summary: Scientists used NASA’s Curiosity Rover to analyze 20 Martian samples, discovering that hematite crystallite size variations could indicate ancient climate changes on Mars.

title: Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope Poster
summary: NASA has released a poster and images for the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope. The materials are available for download in various formats.

title: Contractor to Civil Servant: NASA Welcomes Kenny Heckle
summary: NASA welcomes Kenny Heckle, who grew up near Kennedy Space Center and comes from a family of union pipefitters. The article highlights his background and connection to the space agency.

title: NASA Astronaut Andrew Morgan Retires
summary: NASA astronaut Andrew Morgan retired from the agency on May 28, 2026, after a 12-year career that included 272 days in space aboard the International Space Station. He was part of NASA's 21st astronaut class, selected in 2013.

title: Going Low and Slow in Testing
summary: NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic research aircraft conducted low-speed and low-altitude test flights at Armstrong Flight Research Center in April 2026. The aircraft, part of the Quesst mission, has completed initial high-altitude and near-supersonic speed tests.

title: NASA’s Roman Mission Preps to Unveil New Populations of Faraway Worlds
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope is set to discover around 100,000 exoplanets, significantly expanding the known population of distant worlds. This mission represents a major advancement compared to the approximately 6,300 exoplanets identified by current NASA efforts.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, focusing on deploying rovers and drones to prepare for future lunar habitation. The initiative builds on existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo project among the highlights. The launches are part of a busy manifest dominated by SpaceX's Starlink deployments.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance tasks throughout April. The activities highlight ongoing operations and research in low Earth orbit.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to China's Tiangong space station, which has been continuously occupied since June 5, 2022.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, demonstrating strong performance during its first flight. The mission marked a significant milestone for SpaceX's next-generation rocket.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's Starship Flight 12, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C flight.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship. It highlights the ongoing changes and advancements in this critical component of future space missions.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is actively planning and processing the Artemis III mission, which is a critical crewed mission to low Earth orbit. The mission includes key Human Landing System (HLS) tests.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on space industry news, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of milestones in the European space sector.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and select materials for spacecraft to ensure they withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas to support ESA's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space activities.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling improved handling of objects in orbit. This innovation could enhance satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Observable Space Raises $90M Series A
summary: Observable Space, a space-tech startup, raised $90 million in Series A funding to expand its laser and optical hardware production and international presence beyond the US.

title: Space Markets Emerges From Stealth With Coinbase Ventures Investment
summary: Space Markets has emerged from stealth mode with a new investment from Coinbase Ventures. The company aims to create futures markets for space-related commodities and resources.

title: Exclusive: Stellar Alpina Raises $4.5M to Build Rotating Detonation Engines
summary: Stellar Alpina, a European propulsion startup, has raised $4.5 million to develop rotating detonation engines, a novel technology aimed at advancing spacecraft propulsion. This funding will support the company's efforts to bring this innovative thrust method to the market.

title: Aitech Upgrades its Space Supercomputer
summary: Aitech has upgraded its space supercomputer by integrating NVIDIA's IGX Thor platform into the S-A2300 COTS AI Supercomputer, enhancing in-orbit data processing capabilities for customers.

title: NASA Awards LTV, Lunar Transport Contracts
summary: NASA has awarded contracts for Lunar Terrain Vehicles (LTV) and lunar transport services, marking a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman emphasized the progress during a press conference at NASA HQ.

title: Op-ed: Commercial Demand in LEO is Lacking. The Government Can Help.
summary: A recent op-ed argues that the most promising market opportunity in Low Earth Orbit (LEO)—in-space manufacturing—currently lacks sufficient commercial demand to sustain itself, and suggests government support is necessary to drive growth.

title: Optical Links: When to Incorporate Them? When Not To?
summary: The article discusses the cost-effectiveness of optical links for satellite applications, noting that they often cost hundreds of thousands of dollars and may not be worth the investment. It explores scenarios where optical links are beneficial versus when they are not.

title: Satellogic Lands $18M+ Defense-Monitoring Contract
summary: Satellogic secured a defense-monitoring contract worth over $18 million, marking its first major agreement following a strategic shift to persistent-monitoring capabilities.

title: SpaceX Launches Starship V3 for the First Time
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Starship V3 rocket for the first time, marking its twelfth test flight with a full suite of upgrades. This marks a significant milestone in the development of the next-generation launch vehicle.

title: SEOPS Will Start Waymaker LEO Rideshares In 2028
summary: SEOPS is planning to launch Waymaker LEO rideshares in 2028, targeting time-sensitive or non-standard payloads that current rideshare options cannot accommodate.

title: EU Targets User Terminal Tech Advancements Ahead of IRIS²
summary: The European Union has issued a tender for innovative and cost-effective user terminal technologies for the IRIS² satellite constellation. This move aims to advance user terminal tech ahead of the constellation's deployment.

title: BlackSky Advances Non-Earth Imaging Business With 7-Figure Renewal
summary: BlackSky Technology secured a seven-figure contract renewal for its non-Earth imaging services, which involve capturing imagery of objects in orbit. The company highlighted this award as a significant milestone for its space-based observation capabilities.

title: UK’s O2 Satellite Expands D2D Service to iPhones
summary: UK’s O2 Satellite has expanded its direct-to-device (D2D) service to support iPhones, leveraging SpaceX’s Starlink Mobile constellation. The service was initially launched earlier this year.

title: Swissto12 Adds to Leadership Team to Target Global Growth
summary: Swissto12 has expanded its leadership team with new appointments, including a chief commercial officer and executives focused on government affairs and partnerships, to support its global expansion goals.

title: Orbital Data Centers Must Tackle Chip Lifespan, Launch Availability, and Cybersecurity Challenges
summary: The article discusses the challenges orbital data centers face, including chip lifespan, launch availability, and cybersecurity, particularly in the context of SpaceX's IPO documents. It highlights the future potential of moving compute operations to space.

title: Will the European Launch Market Finally Ignite?
summary: The European space industry is seeking more efficient and reliable access to space, a critical topic discussed in a recent launch panel. The question of when this will be achieved remains a key challenge for the sector.

title: Archangel Lightworks Demonstrates Deployable Optical Ground Station
summary: Archangel Lightworks has successfully completed field tests of its deployable optical ground station Terra-M, enabling data transfer between the ground station and a Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite. This marks a significant advancement in optical communication technology for space applications.

title: Anuvu Taps Eutelsat for IFC Capacity on Eutelsat 10B
summary: Anuvu has signed a multi-year agreement with Eutelsat to use satellite capacity on the Eutelsat 10B satellite for in-flight connectivity (IFC) services. The Eutelsat 10B satellite is positioned at 10 degrees East.

title: European Commission Reserves Portion of Critical MSS Spectrum for EU Operators
summary: The European Commission has outlined a process to allocate licenses for critical mobile satellite services (MSS) spectrum in Europe, prioritizing EU operators. This decision aims to ensure control over key spectrum resources within the region.

title: Space Force Awards SpaceX $2.3 Billion Contract for Space Data Network
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded SpaceX a $2.29 billion contract to develop the Space Data Network (SDN) Backbone, a proliferated Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) system. This contract aims to enhance secure and resilient data transmission for military and government communications.
